checks.a_analysis: The document's content is mapped one-to-one from the source to the output. Each paragraph, the title, and the signature lines appear only once in the JSON, matching their single appearance in the original PDF. There are no instances of duplicated blocks or repeated sections.

The main resolution title is correctly identified as a heading and assigned level 2. While there is no level 1 heading within the sections array, the metadata contains a title which often serves as the primary identifier. Starting the body with an H2 is a common accessibility normalization. There are no skips (e.g., jumping from H2 to H4) or incorrect nesting within the document structure.

The source document does not contain any tables. The header and signature areas, which use a side-by-side visual layout, have been appropriately linearized into paragraphs and images in the output. No table structures were incorrectly created or corrupted.

The output follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the source document. It correctly sequences the header, seal, title, whereas clauses, resolution statement, and final signatures. The side-by-side signature block at the bottom is linearized correctly (signature/name for person A, then signature/name for person B), which is standard for accessibility and logical information flow.

The source document does not contain any formal lists (bulleted or numbered). The series of 'WHEREAS' clauses are correctly rendered as individual paragraphs in the output, preserving their structural meaning without incorrectly formatting them as a list.
There are no hyperlinks present in the source document, and the output correctly reflects this by not including any 'link' types or URI attributes.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the single-column visual organization of the source PDF. The heading, sub-headings, body text (WHEREAS and RESOLVED clauses), and signature blocks are presented in the correct sequence. Differences in font, text alignment, and margins are expected transitions to a responsive HTML format.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable.

The source PDF contains a meaningful official logo (the Mitchell County Seal) and two handwritten signatures which serve as visual evidence of adoption. In the HTML output, these meaningful images are missing and have been replaced by generic, unidentifiable black icons. While these may be placeholders, they fail to present the meaningful visual content (the logo's details and the handwritten nature of the signatures) required for visual fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains a circular colored seal 'MITCHELL COUNTY 1861 NORTH CAROLINA' in the header and two handwritten signatures above the names Taylor McCurry and Steve Pitman.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows a generic black icon at the top of the document where the seal should be, and two similar icons preceding the signature names.
checks.b_evidence_location: Header and signature block at the end of the document.

checks.a_analysis: The document title appears once as a main heading in the HTML, and the body text proceeds without any repetition of paragraphs, signatures, or sections. There is no visual duplication of body content.
